notes: Add new stars here without touching Python logic. Condition types: 'always', 'genre' (genres list), 'size_min' (value), 'ad_slots_min' (value), 'age_min' (value). Effect/fallback fields: v_flat (int), v_mult (float, omit=1.0), income (int), upkeep (int, positive=more cost, negative=discount).

stars.name: Ryan Branston
stars.desc: Sells gin, superhero suits, and everything in between. Brings devastating charm to every role. Also owns a football club.

stars.name: Donny Deviti
stars.desc: Tiny. Chaotic. Shows up unexpectedly in everything since 1975. The fans are thrilled every single time.

stars.name: Chef Gordon Rampsey
stars.desc: Generates so much TV revenue that his salary is technically tax-deductible. THE RISOTTO IS RAW AND SO IS MY DISAPPOINTMENT.

stars.name: Tim Sprint
stars.desc: Does all his own stunts. Every single one. Nobody asked him to. He cannot be stopped. He will not stop.

stars.name: A-List Celeb
stars.desc: Won two Oscars. You've seen every film. Still can't quite remember their name. It's the vibe that counts.

stars.name: Washed-Up Icon
stars.desc: Had ONE viral moment in 2003. Mentions it in every interview. The nostalgia demographic is loyal to a fault.

stars.name: Kieran Royalty
stars.desc: Zero notes from directors. Multiple award-winning. Probably a Roman. Disgusting, and we mean that as the highest compliment.

stars.name: The Pebble
stars.desc: Motivated. Enormously jacked. Owns three production companies. Smells like success and fresh coconut oil.

stars.name: Reality TV Royalty
stars.condition_text: If on REALITY: Views x1.6 & +$8 Income
stars.desc: Built a billion-dollar empire from a single TV appearance. Her publicist earns more than your network's annual budget.

stars.name: Political Satirist
stars.desc: Hosts a fake news show. Somehow better journalism than actual news. Has won a Peabody Award ironically and meant it.

stars.name: Sci-Fi Convention Legend
stars.desc: Has attended every Comic-Con since 1987. The fanbase would literally die for them. Several have tried.

stars.name: Social Media Influencer
stars.condition_text: If on REALITY: +90 Views. Otherwise: -10 Views
stars.desc: 40 million followers. Zero acting range. Somehow this is fine. The engagement metrics are undeniably real.

stars.name: Method Maverick
stars.desc: Stays in character through budget meetings. Craft services now offers emotional support snacks.

stars.name: Sitcom Quipster
stars.desc: Punchline every six seconds. The laugh track union has filed a grievance.

stars.name: Cosmo Captain
stars.desc: Knows every star system and every merch SKU. Insists the ship is real.

stars.name: Reality Gremlin
stars.desc: Thrives on chaos, confessionals, and dramatic music stings.

stars.name: Sports Legend
stars.condition_text: If on SPORTS: Views x1.4 & +80 Views (Upkeep +$10)
stars.desc: Retired jersey, still demands Gatorade baths and prime-time billing.

stars.name: Event-Only Diva
stars.desc: Only shows up for blockbuster nights and demands extra fog machines.

stars.name: Card Shark Cameo
stars.condition_text: If show has 2+ Ad slots: Views x1.3 (Upkeep +$5)
stars.desc: Appears for sponsor-heavy episodes and exits with a suitcase of swag.

stars.name: Streamer Turned Actor
stars.desc: Brings millions of followers and one confusing emote to every scene.

stars.name: Sweeps Whisperer
stars.desc: Knows exactly when to kill a character for ratings.

stars.name: Stunt Casting
stars.desc: Does the big stunts so you don't have to pay for CGI twice.

stars.name: Lucille Ball
stars.condition_text: If on SITCOM: Views x1.6 & +$5 Income (Upkeep +$8)
stars.desc: The original sitcom titan. I Love Lucy reruns still hold the couch. Desi would approve the back-end.

stars.name: Bryan Cranston
stars.condition_text: If on DRAMA: +160 Views & Views x1.2 (Upkeep +$8)
stars.desc: He is the danger. Critics agree. Emmy shelf required.

stars.name: Patrick Stewart
stars.desc: Captain-level gravitas. The Ready Room is now booked solid.

stars.name: Veteran Newscaster
stars.desc: 30 years behind the desk. The credibility is real. The hair is also real, which is frankly impressive.

stars.name: Gordon Flambe
stars.desc: Screams at raw scallops with an intensity previously reserved for geopolitics. The COOKING audience loves every second of it.

stars.name: The Humble Host
stars.desc: Has been on television continuously since 1978. Nobody knows which show is which anymore. The audience stays out of pure loyalty.

stars.name: Ad Magnet
stars.condition_text: If show has 2+ Ad slots: Views x1.3 & +$8 Income
stars.desc: Sponsors adore this face. Cameras adore it more. Place them on an ad-heavy show and every product sells itself.

stars.name: Crossover King
stars.desc: Only shows up for blockbuster two-slot productions. Demands a trailer the size of a second studio and craft services in writing.

stars.name: Daytime Darling
stars.desc: The kind of warmth that sells household products and wins regional Emmy awards simultaneously. Exceptional on light daytime programming.

stars.name: News Anchor Emeritus
stars.condition_text: If on NEWS: Views x1.5 & +$8 Income
stars.desc: Forty years of credibility in one silver-haired package. The demographics follow the hair.

stars.name: The Comeback Kid
stars.desc: Audiences root for any show that survives past season three. They root harder when this star shows up for it.

stars.name: Controversy Machine
stars.desc: Does not know what 'not my place to say' means. The publicist earns hazard pay. The ratings are extraordinary.

stars.name: Network Veteran
stars.condition_text: If show has 1+ Ad slot: Views x1.4 (Upkeep +$10)
stars.desc: Has appeared in so many ads they count as infrastructure. Any show with a sponsor sees a 40% viewing bump on their arrival.

stars.name: Superfan Cameo
stars.desc: Won the fan lottery. Brings a homemade replica prop and needs no direction. The internet clips their scene immediately.
